# Soothemade Conversion Audit

*Generated by a 127-agent workflow. 55 printables audited and adversarially verified, 12 ebooks folded in. 94 high-severity conversion issues found across the catalog; 49 title rewrites were caught and corrected by the adversarial verify pass.*

**Artifacts in this folder:**
- `title-rewrites.csv` / `title-rewrites.md` — every old to new title with keywords + price
- `per-product-issues.md` — the full conversion diagnosis per product
- `bundle-matrix.md` — the bundle line-up
- `pricing-model.md` — reprice table + the high-anchor strategy
- `which-product-guide.md` — the buyer-routing table
- `cannibalization-map.md` — overlap clusters + fixes

---

> **Editor's notes (added by Claude when persisting the workflow output):**
> 1. **The title rewrites are a strong draft, not final copy.** The adversarial verify pass optimized hard for keyword coverage, and roughly a dozen titles over-rotated into Etsy keyword-stuffing that breaks the calm brand voice. Worst offenders: **P03** (ends "Postpartum Journal, Used Badly" — nonsense out of context) and **P31** (uses pipe-stuffing `| ... | ...`). They need a short voice pull-back pass before going live. See `title-rewrites.md`.
> 2. **The megabundle is underpriced as an anchor.** `bundle-matrix.md` lists it at $65 (only ~27% off a-la-carte), which is too small a number to function as a price anchor. This summary's instinct to set the anchor far higher (around $189 against the catalog's ~$752 of single prices) is the correct merchandising call. Raise it.
> 3. Some specific figures in the prose below ("16 kits") are the writer's round numbers; the structured artifacts are authoritative (14 bundles, full reprice table in `pricing-model.md`).

# Soothemade Conversion Audit: Executive Summary

You are about to point Etsy, Pinterest, and Kindle traffic at a shop the search box cannot see. That is the headline. The products are good. The surface they are sold on is the leak. Fix the surface now, before the traffic arrives, or you will pay for clicks that land on listings no one was searching for.

## The one move that matters most: rewrite the titles

Of the 55 printables audited, the top issue on nearly every single one is the same: **the title is built from brand-voice phrases buyers never type.** "Cue Decoder," "Saying No," "Slow Motherhood," "Handoff Pack," "Wait + Welcome." Lovely on the page. Invisible in search. A title rewrite costs about three minutes per product and is the difference between a listing that ranks and one that does not exist to a shopper.

**Start with P01, the sensory deck.** It is your deepest product and your single biggest leak. The listing leads with "The Sensory Play Activity Card Deck" and never says **baby**, **0-3**, or **newborn**, which is exactly what parents type. Retitle it to lead with the buyer phrase, for example: **Baby Sensory Play Cards: 0 to 3 Activities for Newborns**. That one edit, on your priciest single, is the highest revenue-per-minute action in this entire audit.

Then work the rest in this order of search demand. Put the typed phrase first, the format word second (printable, cards, planner, tracker), the brand line last:
- **P09 / P21:** name the lane. P09 owns "mood, first year." P21 owns "the racing 2am nights." Right now they read identical and a buyer picks the cheaper cover.
- **P18:** "toilet learning" to **potty training**. Pure buried volume.
- **P20:** add **miscarriage** and a gift signal. The friend with the card out is searching for someone else.
- **P25, P02, P49:** the word is **scripts** and **what to say**, not "boundary pack."
- **P46 / P50:** the tightest collision in the shop. Lead P46 with **first 90 days / hospital discharge**, P50 with **the long haul**. A caregiver should know from the cover which one is theirs.

This is one weekend of focused editing for all 55, and it is the work that makes every paid and pinned click actually convert.

## Second: raise the prices that are bleeding margin

This is pure money and costs zero production time. You are underpricing your deepest items below thinner siblings, which signals "cheap" on your best work.
- **P49** $7 to $12, **P04** $9 to $14, **P25** $9 to $14, **P32** $9 to $14. These are flagships parked on the floor.
- **P52** and **P48** $12 to $16. Workbook-depth on heavy topics with little honest competition.
- The full reprice table moves about 45 items up a band. Do it in one sitting in the Etsy dashboard.

Anchor it with three numbers, high-first: P01 at $24 as the single ceiling, each bundle showing its add-it-up total beside the kit price, and the megabundle at $189 against $752 on the landing page. The big number makes a $14 single feel like nothing.

## Third: ship the bundles

The 16 themed kits are built and priced at roughly two-thirds of a-la-carte. They lift average order value without one new sticker. The Fourth Trimester and Birth Day kits are your broadest, most-gifted carts, so list those first. The megabundle is the anchor, not the bestseller. Its job is to be the biggest number on the shelf.

## Why the order is what it is

Titles first because no price, bundle, or pin works if the listing never surfaces. Prices second because it is free margin you capture in an afternoon. Bundles third because they need the corrected titles and prices underneath them to read right.

Be honest with yourself about one thing: you have not sold to a single customer yet, which means nothing here is sunk. You get to launch correct instead of fixing it later under live traffic. That is the rare good luck of timing, and it closes the moment you start driving clicks.

## Do this first

1. **Retitle P01** to lead with "baby sensory" and "0-3." Your deepest product, your biggest leak, three minutes.
2. **Retitle the rest**, buyer phrase first, format word second, in the demand order above. One weekend.
3. **Split the lookalike pairs** while you retitle: P09/P21, P46/P50, P02/P25/P49, P33/P45. Same edit, kills cannibalization.
4. **Run the full reprice table** in the Etsy dashboard. One sitting, all margin.
5. **Set the three anchors:** P01 at $24, bundle strike-throughs, megabundle at $189 on the landing.
6. **Publish the bundles**, Fourth Trimester and Birth Day first.
7. **Then, and only then, open the traffic taps:** Pinterest, Etsy ads, KDP. The surface is ready to catch them.
